summary: EU diplomatic missions remain in Kyiv despite Russian warnings. Odessa is fortifying its perimeter with anti‑tank ditches and barbed wire. Russian forces continue air and missile strikes across multiple fronts, including a Su‑30 over Belarus and an Iskander hit on the UN food depot in Dnipro.
key_points: EU embassies stay in Kyiv, with the EU Ambassador stating "The EU is not going anywhere" (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30434).
Odessa is constructing kilometers‑long anti‑tank ditches, dugouts and installing barbed wire on city approaches (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30440).
A Russian Su‑30 is reported operating in Belarusian airspace near Kobrin opposite Volyn region (Source: @eRadarrua/93778).
Ukrainian air defence reported a Shahed drone over Kyiv/Brovary (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67229).
Russian Iskander missile struck the UN World Food Programme depot in Dnipro, destroying food supplies for 130,000 people (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
Russian missile attack on Odessa killed one civilian and injured three, also damaging infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81464).
Russia's State Duma passed a law to seize assets of foreigners convicted of offenses abroad (Source: @tass_agency/377616).

sections.executive_summary: EU diplomatic missions remain in Kyiv despite Russian threats, highlighting political resolve. Odessa is rapidly fortifying its perimeter with anti‑tank obstacles and barbed wire. Russian air activity continues, including a Su‑30 over Belarus and missile strikes on civilian and aid targets.

points: The EU Ambassador confirmed that all EU diplomatic missions will stay in Kyiv, rejecting Russian evacuation warnings (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30434).
Odessa's Southern Defense Forces report completion of kilometers‑long anti‑tank ditches, dugouts, and barbed‑wire installations around city approaches (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30440).
Ukrainian air defence detected a Shahed drone over Kyiv/Brovary, indicating ongoing low‑altitude attack attempts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67229).
The UN World Food Programme depot in Dnipro was hit by an Iskander missile, destroying food stocks intended for 130,000 people (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
A Russian Su‑30 fighter was observed operating in Belarusian airspace near Kobrin, opposite Volyn region (Source: @eRadarrua/93778).
Russian missile strikes on Odessa resulted in one civilian death, three injuries, and damage to a residential area and a school (Source: @UkraineNow/81464).
Russia's State Duma approved a law allowing seizure of assets belonging to foreigners convicted of offenses abroad (Source: @tass_agency/377616).
TASS reported that Russia claims the attack on the Starobelsk college was a planned terrorist act with precise targeting (Source: @tass_agency/377647).
Ukrainian reports indicate multiple UAV movements in Chernihiv and Sumy regions, including UAVs from Bryansk heading to Chernihiv and from Belgorod to southern Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/62867).
A UAV was observed over northern Poltava heading west, and another over central Sumy heading toward Konotop (Source: @kpszsu/62878).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the north‑east direction, posing a threat to front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/62849).
Ukrainian forces reported damage to residential buildings in Poltava region from Russian attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81465).
UAV activity reported from Belarusian border moving westward across Chernihiv region (Source: @kpszsu/62867).
A Russian UAV was sighted over northern Poltava heading west (Source: @kpszsu/62878).
Tactical aviation threats were highlighted for north‑east directions, affecting front‑line zones (Source: @kpszsu/62849).
No specific ground engagements were detailed in the provided messages for the northern front.
Russian missile strike on Zaporizhzhia injured two civilians and a police officer, damaging vehicles and nearby buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81471).
Ukrainian reports of UAVs from Kherson heading toward Kryvyi Rih (Source: @kpszsu/62875).
Russian attacks on Odessa caused civilian casualties and infrastructure damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81464).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV group from Kursk region moving toward Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/62862).
Russian Su‑30 fighter observed over Belarus near Kobrin (Source: @eRadarrua/93778).
Ukrainian air defence intercepted a Shahed drone over Kyiv/Brovary (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67229).
Tactical aviation activity reported on north‑east and south‑east directions, indicating potential strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/62849, @kpszsu/62843).
Ukrainian officials warned of a serious shortage of air‑defence missiles (Source: @tass_agency/377650).

key_developments: EU diplomatic missions will remain in Kyiv, rejecting Russian evacuation threats (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30434).
Odessa is finalising kilometers‑long anti‑tank ditches, dugouts and barbed‑wire perimeters (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30440).
A Russian Su‑30 fighter is operating in Belarusian airspace near Kobrin (Source: @eRadarrua/93778).
Ukrainian air defence reported a Shahed drone over Kyiv/Brovary (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67229).
Russian Iskander missile struck the UN World Food Programme depot in Dnipro, destroying supplies for 130,000 people (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
Russian missile attack on Odessa killed one civilian and injured three, also damaging a school (Source: @UkraineNow/81464).
Russia passed a law to seize assets of foreigners convicted of offenses abroad (Source: @tass_agency/377616).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces continue to project power across multiple domains, employing air assets such as Su‑30 fighters over Belarus and missile strikes on civilian and humanitarian targets, indicating an intent to pressure both military and civilian morale. Ukrainian defensive measures, including extensive fortifications around Odessa and active air defence against Shahed drones, show adaptation but face material shortages, especially in air‑defence missiles. The persistence of UAV incursions across the eastern and southern fronts suggests ongoing reconnaissance and strike capabilities that could facilitate future artillery or missile operations.
sections.regional_response: EU member states reaffirm their diplomatic presence in Kyiv, signaling political support despite Russian intimidation. Ukraine's local authorities are accelerating defensive construction in key southern cities like Odessa, while international aid facilities such as the UN WFP depot remain vulnerable to Russian strikes, potentially prompting reassessment of aid security protocols. Russian legislative actions targeting foreign assets may aim to deter external support for Ukraine, but could also strain diplomatic relations with Western nations.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths reveals a pattern of cross‑border movements from Russian‑occupied territories into Ukrainian heartland, necessitating enhanced radar coverage. The reported shortage of air‑defence missiles could limit Ukraine's ability to counter high‑speed missile threats, highlighting a need for accelerated resupply. Russian use of precision‑guided Iskander missiles against humanitarian targets underscores a strategy of targeting logistics to undermine civilian resilience.
